data.title: CVB Financial Q1 net interest income rises
data.description:
Overview

  • California commercial bank reported Q1 net income nearly flat yr/yr, EPS rose to $0.38

  • Net interest income grew 6.7% yr/yr, driven by higher average earning assets and loan yields

  • Company completed Heritage Commerce Corp acquisition on April 17, results to be included in Q2

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance for the current or future periods in the press release

Result Drivers

  • NET INTEREST INCOME GROWTH - Higher average interest-earning assets and increased loan yields drove 6.7% yr/yr growth in net interest income

  • LOWER FUNDING COSTS - Decreased cost of funds and deposits supported margin expansion

  • HIGHER CREDIT LOSS PROVISION - Provision for credit losses increased due to higher specific reserves for one commercial and industrial credit

data.title: ServiceNow Posts 22% 1Q Revenue Growth, But Says Armis Deal Will Weigh on Margins
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

ServiceNow posted 22% revenue growth in the first quarter but projected the integration of Armis weighing on its margins this year.

The cloud-based software company said Wednesday that the acquisition of Armis, a cybersecurity startup, will result in a roughly 75 basis point headwind to its operating margin for the full year, as well as a roughly 125 basis point headwind to its operating margin in the second quarter.

It is also projecting 25 basis point headwind to full-year subscription gross margin and approximately a 200 basis point headwind to free cash flow margins for the year.

The company guided for full-year subscription revenue between $15.74 billion and $15.78 billion.

That projection fell short of the $15.98 billion Wall Street analysts are expecting, according to FactSet.

"While we will see some near-term headwinds to margins as we integrate the business in FY 2026, strong AI efficiencies internally from Now on Now and our underlying platform leverage are expected to normalize our operating and free cash flow margin expansion trajectories in FY 2027," the company said.

Shares of ServiceNow tumbled 12% in after-hours trading to $90.20. The stock closed up 2.9% at $103.07, down 33% this year.

For the current second quarter, ServiceNow is expecting subscription revenue to grow to roughly $3.82 billion, with current remaining performance obligations expected to rise 19%.

Analysts are expecting $3.86 billion in revenue.

"The early close of our Armis acquisition meaningfully expands our TAM and accelerates our subscription revenue growth trajectory," Chief Financial Officer Gina Mastantuono said.

ServiceNow recorded a first-quarter profit of $469 million, or 45 cents a share, compared with a profit of $460 million, or 44 cents a share, a year earlier.

On an adjusted basis, ServiceNow posted earnings of 97 cents a share, in line with the expectations of analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose to $3.77 billion from $3.09 billion, ahead of analysts' projections of $3.75 billion.

"As new technologies create both opportunity and risk, our two decades of engineering combined with deep business context enable us to orchestrate and secure the agentic enterprise," ServiceNow Chief Executive Officer Bill McDermott said. "With this foundation, our AI growth is far exceeding even our own expectations, reinforcing our position as one of the fastest growing enterprise software companies ever."

The company ended the quarter with $12.64 billion in current remaining performance obligations, up nearly 23% year-over-year.

Subscription revenues rose 22% to $3.67 billion. Subscription revenue growth faced a 75 basis point headwind from the delayed closings of several Middle East deals, which were disrupted by the war in Iran, the company said.

ServiceNow's outlook "reflects a prudent assessment of those geopolitical headwinds on deal timing," the company said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: S&P and Nasdaq Set Fresh Records After Cease-Fire Extension — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Erin Lang

Stock investors are willing to bet the worst of the U.S. war with Iran is in the past.

All three major U.S. stock indexes climbed Wednesday after President Trump said late Tuesday that he would extend a cease-fire with Iran. The S&P 500 notched its eighth record close of 2026.

The gains extend a rally that has propelled the index to multiple all-time highs in recent trading sessions, even as Iran attacks ships in the Strait of Hormuz and a more permanent peace between the two nations remains far from certain.

The president's cease-fire extension "doesn't really bring any clarity, but for now it's good enough for the market," said Adam Turnquist, chief technical strategist at LPL Financial. "Risk appetite is back."

The S&P 500 rose 1%, while the Nasdaq composite jumped 1.6%, closing at its fourth record high of the year. The Dow industrials advanced 0.7%, or 341 points.

Oil traders, on the other hand, don't appear so convinced. Global benchmark crude prices rose 3.5% to $101.91 a barrel Wednesday, settling above $100 for the first time in two weeks.

While the aerial war between the U.S. and Iran is on pause, the fight for control of the strait, a crucial waterway for global energy exports, continues.

The U.S. is expected to maintain economic pressure on Iran, according to U.S. officials, including sanctions, a blockade of Iranian ports and an effort to seize Iran-linked ships in waters around the world. Even if ship traffic resumes, many analysts believe energy prices will be higher for the rest of the year.

None of that has stopped traders from piling into stocks. With equity investors growing more optimistic that the conflict will end, many have turned their attention instead to companies' first-quarter earnings updates. So far, those reports have painted a relatively upbeat picture of the American economy: Business at the country's biggest banks is booming, and company executives have indicated that consumers are still spending.

The good news continued to roll in this week: Boeing shares rose 5.5% after the plane maker narrowed its quarterly loss and logged brisk commercial-jet sales. Tesla shares jumped post-market after the company beat revenue expectations.

"Investors have come back to the fundamentals, now that the headlines are calming down a bit," said Brian Vendig, chief investment officer at MJP Wealth Advisors.

Rising shares of large tech companies including Microsoft, Amazon and Alphabet juiced gains in the major indexes Wednesday. Those stocks have helped lead the market higher in recent sessions, as investors reconsider the titans of the artificial intelligence trade. Smaller tech names also rose: Micron Technology surged 8.5% Wednesday, while flash memory maker Sandisk Corp jumped 8.4%.

Earlier this year, traders flocked out of richly-priced technology names for stocks in the industrials, materials and similar sectors. The reasoning: those companies could benefit from broadening economic growth — or, in some cases, might be the most insulated from any disruption caused by the advent of AI.

That rotation has been put on pause, and the AI trade is back in the spotlight. Megacap tech companies are still expected to be significant contributors to first-quarter earnings growth. Lower valuations are also luring investors, after the war in Iran and a fresh round of AI jitters battered tech stocks in the first quarter.

"Tech is catching a bid this month because it got beaten up so much in the first few months of the year," Vendig said.

Write to Hannah Erin Lang at hannaherin.lang@wsj.com

data.title: TE Connectivity Beats Earnings Estimates. The Stock Is Falling. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

TE Connectivity reported better-than-expected quarterly numbers. The artificial-intelligence boom is alive and well, and other end markets are joining the party.

TE stock, however, was plunging, with the reason hard to suss out.

The electrical component supplier reported fiscal second-quarter earnings per share of $2.73 from sales of $4.7 billion on Wednesday. Wall Street was looking for $2.69 a share from sales of $4.7 billion. Results topped TE's own guidance of $2.65 a share.

A year ago, TE reported EPS of $1.86 from sales of $3.97 billion.

Looking ahead, the company expects fiscal third-quarter earnings per share of $2.83, up 17% from the same period a year earlier, from sales of $5 billion. Wall Street projects earnings of $2.80 a share and revenue of $4.9 billion.

"This performance and our record orders were driven by our strategic positioning in key trends, including AI, next-generation transportation, and electric grid modernization, along with the broadening of growth across our portfolio," said CEO Terrence Curtin in a statement.

Energy, aerospace & defense, factory automation, and industrial transportation businesses are picking up, Curtin told Barron's. Orders in the quarter were a record $5.3 billion, up 25% year over year. The broadening of growth is a positive data point for other industrial stocks.

Operating profit margins were strong as well, at 22% in the quarter, up more than one percentage point year over year. TE's AI business will be $2.3 billion or $2.4 billion in fiscal year 2026, up from about $900 million in fiscal year 2025.

TE stock was up 1% in premarket trading. Gains faded, though. Shares were at $218.56 in early trading, off 10.1%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were up 0.7% and 0.8%, respectively.

Exactly why shares are getting hammered is hard to say. The quarter looks fine. — and TE stock typically isn't that volatile on earnings. Shares fell 1% after the December quarter report and rose 2% after the September quarter report.

Expectations may have been running high. Coming into Wednesday trading, TE stock was up about 7% year to date and up about 89% over the past 12 months. Those gains left shares trading for about 21 times earnings expected over the next 12 months, up from about 16 times a year ago.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Blue Bird to Report Fiscal 2026 Second Quarter Results on May 6, 2026
data.description:
Webcast and Conference Call Scheduled for 4:30PM ET

MACON, Ga.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 22, 2026--

Blue Bird Corporation , the leader in electric and cleaner-emission school buses, will release its fiscal 2026 second quarter results on May 6, 2026.

The public is invited to attend an audio webcast in which Blue Bird executives John Wyskiel, President and CEO, and Razvan Radulescu, CFO, will discuss results. This webcast will take place at 4:30PM ET on May 6, 2026. A slide presentation will be available to support the webcast.

Dial-in details and the webcast of the presentation will be available on the Investor Relations portion of Blue Bird's website at http://investors.blue-bird.com. Please click on the link in the Events box in the lower right corner of the Blue Bird Investor Relations landing page to access the webcast.

A replay of the webcast will be available approximately two hours after the call concludes via the same link on Blue Bird's website.

About Blue Bird Corporation

Blue Bird is recognized as a technology leader and innovator of school buses since its founding in 1927. Our dedicated team members design, engineer and manufacture school buses with a singular focus on safety, reliability, and durability. School buses carry the most precious cargo in the world — 25 million children twice a day — making them the most trusted mode of student transportation. The company is the proven leader in low- and zero-emission school buses with more than 25,000 propane, natural gas, and electric powered buses sold. Blue Bird is transforming the student transportation industry through cleaner energy solutions. For more information on Blue Bird's complete product and service portfolio, visit www.blue-bird.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260422163917/en/

CONTACT: Mark Benfield

Blue Bird Corporation

(478) 822-2315

Mark.Benfield@blue-bird.com

data.title: Lululemon Appoints Heidi O'Neill As CEO
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Lululemon Athletica Inc. (LULU) said on Wednesday it has appointed Heidi O'Neill as its next Chief Executive Officer, effective September 8, 2026, following a comprehensive search process.

O'Neill, a longtime Nike Inc. (NKE) executive, brings over three decades of experience in brand strategy, product innovation, and global operations. She will also join the company's Board of Directors upon assuming the role.

She most recently served as President, Consumer, Product & Brand at Nike, where she played a key role in scaling the business and strengthening its global consumer engagement.

'lululemon is an iconic brand with something rare: genuine guest love, a product ethos rooted in innovation, and a global platform still in the early stages of its potential,' said Ms. O'Neill. 'As I step into the CEO role in September, my job will be to build on that foundation - to accelerate product breakthroughs, deepen the brand's cultural relevance, and unlock growth in markets around the world. I am humbled by the opportunity and energized by what the team is already building. I look forward to joining the company and helping to define and deliver the organization's next chapter of success.'

Meghan Frank and André Maestrini will continue to lead as interim co-CEOs until Ms. O'Neill joins the company. At that time, Ms. Frank and Mr. Maestrini plan to return to their previous senior leadership roles and work closely with Ms. O'Neill and the rest of the leadership team to drive momentum forward.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: ASML Stock Drops After TSMC Says No To Latest Lithography Machines
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSMC will not be opting for ASML’s expensive new lithography machines in a cost saving move.
  • TSMC also launched its own A13 and N2U technologies, which will be used in chipmaking. 
  • TSMC’s new chip technology to act as a competition to ASML’s existing tech.

ASML stock dropped 1% on Wednesday after TSMC showcased their newest generation of chip manufacturing ‌technology and planned to hold off on deploying or purchasing ASML’s new lithography machines used for chip production through 2029.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC), which is the world’s largest chipmaker and the supplier to Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL) and Google (GOOGL), launched two chipmaking technologies geared towards AI development named A13 and N2U earlier in the day.

The A13 will go into production in ​2029 and the N2U, which is a more affordable option, will be used to make chips for phones and laptops. TSMC is ASML’s largest customer, according to Bloomberg’s supply chain data.

TSMC’s Deputy Co-Chief Operating Officer, Kevin Zhang, told Bloomberg on Wednesday that the company has no plans to purchase a newer generation of ASML’s high numerical aperture extreme ultraviolet lithography machines or simply "high NA" EUV machines owing to their extremely high cost. One of these ASML machines costs upwards of $400 million.

The move comes at a time when chipmakers are pivoting toward simplified process flows and advanced packaging rather than relying solely on expensive hardware.

What Is ASML’s New EUV Machine?

The double-decker-bus-sized machine is designed to produce the next generation of AI-powered chips as ASML looks to capitalize on the surging demand for semiconductors. Intel (INTC) is the first to deploy it for high-volume manufacturing.

The global semiconductor market is projected to rise 22% to $772 billion this year and more than 25% to $975 billion next year, according to the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics organization.

What Are Analysts Saying?

43 out of 44 analysts rate ASML stock ‘buy’ or higher, with one ‘sell’ recommendation.

RBC Capital analyst Srini Pajjuri raised the firm's price target on ASML to $1,700 from $1,625 and keeps an ‘Outperform’ rating on the shares, and Wells Fargo raised the firm's price target to $1,750 from $1,650 and maintained an ‘Overweight’ rating on the shares last week.

The Retail Take

One user on Stocktwits expressed confidence in the stock by saying there is a “lack of panic”.

https://stocktwits.com/Milli13/message/651058814

Another user highlighted TSMC will have to buy the machine eventually from ASML.

https://stocktwits.com/Milli13/message/651058814

The stock has gained 35% year-to-date.

data.title: ServiceNow Secures $4 Billion Short-Term Unsecured Loan Led by JPMorgan to Fund Armis Deal
data.description:
ServiceNow entered into a Term Loan Credit Agreement for an unsecured $4 billion term loan maturing on Oct 16, 2026, to help finance its acquisition of Armis Security. The loan bears interest at either an alternate base rate or term SOFR plus a margin tied to ServiceNow's credit ratings. The company may extend the maturity by up to six months, subject to lender consent, and the facility includes customary covenants and fees. Management expects the financing to provide timely liquidity for the transaction.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: $4 billion unsecured term loan credit agreement
  • Counterparty: JPMorgan Chase Bank, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Oct 16 2026; optional 6-month extension
  • Reason: Finance part of Armis Security acquisition

Original SEC Filing: ServiceNow, Inc. [ NOW ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Fulton Financial reports Q1 2026 net income $92.2M; operating EPS $0.55, NIM 3.58%
data.description:
Fulton Financial reported net income available to common shareholders of $92.2 million, or $0.51 per diluted share, for the first quarter of 2026 and operating net income available to common shareholders of $99.7 million, or $0.55 per diluted share. Net interest income was $262.0 million with a net interest margin of 3.58%; provision for credit losses was $14.4 million. The quarter included integration activity from the completed Blue Foundry merger and share repurchases of 1.21 million shares for $24.5 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income available to common shareholders: $92.2 million; diluted EPS $0.51 for Q1 2026.
  • Operating net income available to common shareholders: $99.7 million; operating diluted EPS $0.55 for Q1 2026.
  • Net interest income: $262.0 million; net interest margin: 3.58% (down 1 bp from prior quarter).
  • Provision for credit losses: $14.4 million; allowance for credit losses on net loans $367.5 million (1.51% of total net loans).
  • Non-interest expense: $200.3 million; operating non-interest expense: $190.7 million. Share repurchases: 1,212,650 shares for $24.5 million under 2026 repurchase program.

Business Highlights

  • Completed merger with Blue Foundry Bancorp effective April 1, 2026; Blue Foundry Bank will operate as a separate subsidiary until its systems conversion and subsequent merger into Fulton Bank in summer 2026.
  • Total net loans increased to $24.27 billion, up $121.5 million from December 31, 2025, driven by growth in consumer and commercial loan categories and an opportunistic in‑market commercial loan portfolio purchase.
  • Deposits increased to $26.77 billion, a rise of $178.9 million versus December 31, 2025, with growth in savings and noninterest-bearing demand balances partially offset by declines in interest-bearing demand and brokered deposits.
  • Asset quality remained stable: non-performing assets declined to $177.5 million (0.55% of total assets) and annualized net charge-offs were 0.25% of average loans for the quarter.
  • Ongoing integration and strategic priorities focused on seamless customer transition, systems conversion timing for Blue Foundry Bank, and delivering positive operating leverage following the acquisition.

Original SEC Filing: FULTON FINANCIAL CORP [ FULT ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Farmers & Merchants reports Q1 net income $9.6M; EPS $0.70, ROAA 1.14%
data.description:
Farmers & Merchants reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $9.6 million, or $0.70 per basic and diluted share, a 37.8% increase year-over-year. The bank posted a return on average assets of 1.14% and improved net interest margin to 3.42%, driven by a 39-basis-point expansion. Deposits rose to $2.81 billion and loans, net increased to $2.68 billion as of March 31, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $9.6 million for Q1 2026, up 37.8% year-over-year; basic and diluted EPS: $0.70.
  • Net interest margin: 3.42% for the quarter (increase of 39 basis points year-over-year).
  • Return on average assets (ROAA): 1.14% for the quarter.
  • Total deposits: $2.81 billion at March 31, 2026 (up 4.1% year-over-year).
  • Total loans, net: $2.68 billion at March 31, 2026 (up 3.9% year-over-year).

Business Highlights

  • Maintained 92 consecutive quarters of profitability, reflecting long-term operational stability.
  • Executed a new three-year growth-oriented strategic plan focused on core deposit generation, treasury management and retail offerings.
  • Proactive credit management: several watch-list credits were resolved during the quarter and nonperforming loans remained relatively stable quarter-over-quarter.
  • Loan pipeline strength: approximately $60 million in approved loans in the pipeline, indicating continued underlying loan demand.
  • Commercial real estate portfolio remained well-balanced, representing 49.0% of the total loan portfolio with diversified categories including industrial, multi-family, retail and hotels.

Original SEC Filing: FARMERS & MERCHANTS BANCORP INC [ FMAO ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

  • Company will release Q1 2026 earnings after market close May 6 and host a conference call May 7 at 9:00 a.m. ET.
  • Press release furnished as Exhibit 99.1 under Regulation FD; information is furnished, not filed.

Original SEC Filing: CrossAmerica Partners LP [ CAPL ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Supreme Court Rules Against Enbridge in Michigan Effort to Close Line 5 Pipeline — OPIS
data.description:
The Supreme Court on Wednesday ruled against Enbridge's efforts to move a case seeking to shut down the Line 5 pipeline from Michigan state court.

In a unanimous decision, the high court ruled the Canadian company had waited too long to seek the move to a federal court. While the law allows for case participants to seek a change of venue within 30 days of a case being filed, Enbridge waited 887 days, according to the decision by Justice Sonia Sotomayor.

"The Court of Appeals therefore correctly held that Enbridge's notice of removal was untimely and that this action must be remanded to the Michigan state court," Sotomayor wrote in the 18-page decision.

The rulin is a defeat for Enbridge, which has long argued that its operation of the 540,000 b/d pipeline is a federal, not state, matter. Enbridge says federal law and a treaty with Canada govern pipeline operations and Michigan does not have the authority to order a shutdown.

Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel, a Democrat who had campaigned on a pledge to shut the pipeline, is seeking to close an underwater section of Line 5 in the Straits of Mackinac. Nessel argues Enbridge has violated the terms of its state lease on the property and that the pipeline poses the risk of a catastrophic spill into the Great Lakes.

Nessel filed suit in state court in 2019, but that case was put on hold after Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer filed her own suit against the pipeline.

Whitmer's case was eventually moved to federal court, where a judge in December dismissed it.

Enbridge had also been successful in moving Nessel's case to federal court, but the Sixth Circuit Court of Appeals ordered it sent back to state court because Enbridge had missed the 30-day deadline. Enbridge appealed the ruling to the high court, which heard arguments in February.

The pipeline supplies light crude and NGLs to refineries in the Midwest and Canada.

The possibility of a shutdown disrupting such supplies led the Canadian government to invoke its treaty with the United States governing pipeline operations.

Those international considerations mean that even if Enbridge were to lose the case in Michigan court, it would likely appeal such a ruling in federal court.

During the February arguments, the high court justices had questioned attorneys representing Michigan over whether Nessel would immediately seek to shut the pipeline if she won the state court case. The attorneys said they couldn't say.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Steve Cronin, scronin@opisnet.com; Editing by Michael Kelly, mkelly@opisnet.com

data.title: Law grad employment hit record high again in 2025, ABA data shows
data.description:
By Karen Sloan

A record percentage of U.S. law school students landed legal jobs soon after graduating in 2025, according to data released Wednesday by the American Bar Association, despite reduced hiring for government and public interest jobs.

The percentage of 2025 juris doctor graduates who landed jobs requiring bar admission within 10 months of leaving campus ticked up one percent from 2024, which was also a record year, to 83%.

The juris doctor class of 2025 benefited from competition among employers for a smaller pool of fresh graduates. American Bar Association-accredited law schools graduated 2,713 fewer students last year — the result of COVID-19 pandemic enrollment spike among the 2024 graduating class.

“We continue to see a strong job market for graduates” said Jenn Rosato Perea, the ABA’s managing director of accreditation and legal education.

Strong entry level employment among J.D. graduates is among the factors experts say are contributing to a boom in law school applications — which were up 18% nationwide in 2025 and are currently up another 10% this year. The political landscape and recent prominence of lawyers is also pushing people toward law school, they say.

Some experts have warned that the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence by law firms and other legal employers will reduce entry level lawyer hiring in the coming years. The current job numbers don’t yet reflect any declines.

For jobs that either require bar admission or for which a J.D. provides an advantage, the employment rate was nearly 88% in 2025, up slightly from 87% the previous year, the ABA data show. A recent change in how the ABA reports positions funded directly by law schools accounted for that difference, the organization said.

There were 632 fewer 2025 law graduates in government positions — a nearly 14% decline from 2024, according to the ABA data. Some third-year law students saw their permanent job offers revoked amid a widespread federal government hiring freeze imposed by President Donald Trump within days of his return to the White House in January of 2025, though some of those positions were later restored.

Public interest positions were also down nearly 15%, with 513 fewer 2025 graduates in those jobs than the previous year.

The percentage of 2025 graduates who were unemployed and seeking work held steady at nearly 5%, the ABA data show.

Read more:

Politics and the job market made law school a hot ticket in 2025

Law grad job rates surged to historic highs in 2024, ABA data shows

data.title: Raymond James Earnings Beat Forecasts. The Stock Is Falling. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Raymond James Financial reported fiscal second-quarter adjusted net income and revenue that beat Wall Street forecasts thanks to growth in asset-management fees. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of $2.83, higher than analyst forecasts of $2.76, according to FactSet. For the same period a year ago, Raymond James reported adjusted earnings of $2.42.

Revenue rose 13% year over year to $3.86 billion, according to the company. Analysts were expecting revenue of $3.85 billion, according to FactSet.

Shares of the wealth management company were down 1% in afterhours trading. The stock is down 3.7% this year. The benchmark S&P 500 index is up 4.1%.

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: IBM Posts Higher 1Q Sales, Buoyed by AI — Update
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

IBM reported rising revenue and a higher profit in the first quarter, buoyed by the growing adoption of artificial-intelligence tools by businesses.

"AI continues to be a tailwind for our business," IBM Chief Financial Officer Jim Kavanaugh said in an interview. "You see it play out in the results, as we captured demand for both technology and innovation around AI, but also services that help organizations orchestrate, deploy, govern, scale AI across their journeys."

The technology company on Wednesday reported a first-quarter profit of $1.22 billion, or $1.28 a share, compared with a profit of $1.06 billion, or $1.14 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company logged adjusted earnings of $1.91 a share, ahead of Wall Street's expectation of $1.81 a share, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose to $15.92 billion from $14.54 billion a year prior, amounting to what Kavanaugh said was IBM's highest first-quarter revenue growth in many years. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting revenue of $15.63 billion.

Despite the quarterly earnings beat, IBM maintained its expectations of constant currency revenue growth of at least 5% this year, with free cash flow rising by around $1 billion.

Shares fell about 6.4% in late trading to $235.82. Through Wednesday's close, the stock had lost nearly 15% this year.

Revenue in the company's software segment rose to $7.05 billion, up 11%. Within that segment, hybrid cloud revenues, which includes the company's Red Hat business, were up 13%, while automation revenue rose 10% and data revenue rose 19%.

Consulting revenue rose 4% to $5.27 billion, and infrastructure revenue was up 15% to $3.33 billion.

Free cash flow in the quarter was $2.2 billion, up around $300 million year over year. Analysts were expecting $2.04 billion.

IBM's board of directors also increased the company's quarterly dividend to $1.69 a share, up from $1.68.

The new payout, equal to $6.76 a year, represents a 2.6% annual yield based on IBM's Tuesday closing price of $255.68.

The dividend is payable June 10 to shareholders of record as of May 8.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: WEX Q1 revenue rises on Corporate Payments unit growth
data.description:
Overview

  • Global payments solutions provider's Q1 revenue rose 5.8%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted net income per diluted share rose 18.2% year-over-year

  • Company raised full-year 2026 revenue and adjusted net income guidance

Outlook

  • WEX raises full-year 2026 revenue guidance to $2.82 bln-$2.88 bln

  • Company expects full-year 2026 adj net income of $18.95-$19.55 per diluted share

  • WEX sees Q2 2026 revenue of $727 mln-$747 mln and adj net income of $4.93-$5.13 per share

Result Drivers

  • SEGMENT GROWTH - Revenue gains were led by 9.3% growth in Corporate Payments and 8.5% growth in Benefits segments, while Mobility segment revenue rose 3.2%

  • FUEL PRICE AND FX IMPACTS - Revenue was favorably impacted by $5.5 mln from U.S. fuel prices and $5.1 mln from foreign exchange rates, but offset by a $7.6 mln negative impact from international fuel price spreads internationally

  • AI AND OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY - Ongoing integration of AI into operations and product development aimed at accelerating efficiency and improving payment workflows, per CEO Melissa Smith
